dependencies: Network of about 3,700 third-party repair shops for vehicle claims
Network of independent claim field adjusters for personal property claims
Third-party systems and applications for business operations
Cloud computing environments and software as a service applications
risks: Reliance on third-party systems - interruption or degradation of service from third-party systems could impair critical business functions
Vendor concentration - determination by a vendor to abandon or terminate support for a system, product, obligation, or service significant to business
Third-party vendor cybersecurity risks - increased risk of loss, corruption, or unauthorized access to data through vendor connections
Vendor lax security measures, data location uncertainty, and data storage in inappropriate jurisdictions
Competitive labor markets causing increased costs for third-party labor
Loss of access to qualified external talent on a cost-effective basis

regulatory_risks: Insurance regulation and supervision by insurance departments in all 50 states, District of Columbia, Bermuda, and Canada
Licensing requirements for insurers and agents
Capital and surplus requirements
Restrictions on marketing practices
Statutory accounting principles and reporting requirements
Requirements for establishing insurance reserves
Investment restrictions and limitations
Acquisitions of insurers and transactions between insurers and affiliates regulated
Limitations on rates of return or profitability
Rating criteria, rate levels, and rate changes subject to regulatory approval
Insolvency regulations and guaranty fund assessments
Assigned risk programs and involuntary market plans
Authority to exit a business subject to regulatory approval
Required coverages, policy forms, policy cancellations and non-renewals regulated
Underwriting standards and claims handling requirements
Rate regulation varies from 'use and file' to 'file and use' to 'prior approval'
Laws and regulations limiting use of specific rating factors (credit, education, occupation)
Data privacy and security laws and regulations with complex compliance and reporting requirements
Cybersecurity regulations and regulatory expectations
NAIC AI guiding principles and model bulletin adopted by nearly half of state insurance departments
State AI legislation and regulations on use of artificial intelligence and algorithms
Laws regarding unfair claims and trade practices applicable to AI activities
Federal laws on use of credit information, methods of customer communications, employment practices, medical cost reimbursement
Risk-based capital (RBC) program requirements
Holding company laws regulating dividends and distributions between insurers and affiliates
Laws limiting ability to cancel or non-renew policies
Laws prohibiting withdrawal from markets without approved plan
Potential federal regulation of insurance industry
Federal Insurance Office monitoring for systemic risk
ESG and sustainability regulations potentially narrowing universe of permitted securities
Regulations on environmental, social, and governance investment disclosures
